DTR  |  Listen to a Classroom Conversation
LCC-MS7-L1-13001400-MSELA7-DBT-GBL-01 • MS ELA Grade 7 • Analyze Use of Text Features in Public Documents

The Hands That Hold the Sign

Click  🎧  to listen.
Listen to a conversation between a girl and a boy. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer some questions.
Listen to the Conversation
1QUESTION 1: What are the students mainly doing in the conversation?
2QUESTION 2: Why does the boy think the three stacked languages do the most work?
3QUESTION 3: What will the students probably do next?
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
Answer all 3 questions to submit.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Locate the line that names the poster's audience. Locate the line that gives the boy's reason for the languages. Locate the line where the students name their next step.
CA ELD PI.A.1, PI.A.3, PI.B.5  |  CCSS RI.7.5  |  MS7 MSELA7 — Analyze Use of Text Features in Public Documents — The Hands That Hold the Sign
